org-sec-internal.label: Organization of information security - Internal Organization

org-sec-mobile.label: Organization of information security - Mobile devices and teleworking

hr-pre-employment.label: Human Resources Security - Prior to employment

hr-termination-change.label: Human Resources Security - Termination and change of employment

access-business-req.label: Access Control - Business requirements of access control

access-sys-app.label: Access Control - System and application access control

physical-secure-areas.label: Physical and Environmental Security - Secure Areas

ops-procedures.label: Operations security - Operational procedures and responsibilities

ops-software-control.label: Operations security - Control of operational software

ops-audit-controls.label: Operations security - Information systems audit controls

sys-acq-requirements.label: System acquisition, development and maintenance - Security requirements of information systems

sys-acq-dev-support.label: System acquisition, development and maintenance - Security in development and support processes

sys-acq-test-data.label: System acquisition, development and maintenance - Test data

supplier-delivery.label: Supplier relationships - Supplier service delivery management

incident-mgmt.label: Information security incident management - Management of information security incidents and improvements

bcm-continuity.label: Information security aspects of business continuity management - Information security continuity

bcm-redundancies.label: Information security aspects of business continuity management - Redundancies

compliance-legal.label: Compliance - Compliance with legal and contractual requirements

iso-2013-a-5-1-1.requirements: A set of policies for information and cybersecurity shall be defined, approved by C-level, published and communicated to employees and relevant external parties.

iso-2013-a-5-1-2.control: Review of the policies for information security
iso-2013-a-5-1-2.requirements: The policies for information and cybersecurity shall be reviewed at planned intervals or if significant changes occur to ensure their continuing suitability, adequacy, communication and effectiveness.

iso-2013-a-6-1-1.requirements: All information and cybersecurity roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated.

iso-2013-a-6-1-2.requirements: Conflicting tasks and areas of responsibility should be separated to reduce opportunities to change or misuse the assets of the organization without permission or unintended.

iso-2013-a-6-1-3.requirements: It is necessary to maintain proper communications with the relevant authorities.

iso-2013-a-6-1-4.requirements: Appropriate connections should be established with special interest organizations or other forums for professional security and professional associations.

iso-2013-a-6-1-5.requirements: Throughout project management, the confidentiality of information should be discussed irrespective of project type

iso-2013-a-6-2-1.requirements: To manage the risks introduced by the use of mobile devices, a policy and supporting safety measures should be adopted.

iso-2013-a-6-2-2.requirements: To guard the accessed, processed, or stored information at teleworking sites, a policy and supporting security measures should be implemented.

iso-2013-a-7-1-1.requirements: Background verification checks on all job applicants will be performed in compliance with applicable rules, legislation, and ethics and should be proportionate to business criteria, classification of the information to be obtained, and potential risks.

iso-2013-a-7-1-2.requirements: Contract agreements with employees and contractors would set out their responsibility for information security, and hence the responsibility of the company.

iso-2013-a-7-2-1.requirements: Management should mandate all employees and contractors to exercise information security in accordance with established policies and procedures set by the organization.

iso-2013-a-7-2-2.control: Information security awareness, education and training
iso-2013-a-7-2-2.requirements: All company workers and, where necessary, contractors will receive adequate awareness education and training, as well as daily updates on organizational policies and procedures, as applicable to their job function.

iso-2013-a-7-2-3.requirements: A formal and informed administrative process will be in place to take action against employees who have committed an information security breach.

iso-2013-a-7-3-1.control: Termination or change of employment responsibilities
iso-2013-a-7-3-1.requirements: Responsibility and information/cyber security requirements that continue to be valid following termination or change of employment must be defined, communicated to, and implemented by the employee or contractor.

iso-2013-a-8-1-1.requirements: Assets related to information and information facilities of an organization should be identified and listed, inventory of these assets should also be maintained.

iso-2013-a-8-1-2.requirements: Assets in the inventory should have their owners (Asset-owner)

iso-2013-a-8-1-3.requirements: Rules should be identified, documented, and implemented for the acceptable use of information and assets linked to information and information processing facilities.

iso-2013-a-8-1-4.requirements: Both workers and external stakeholders must return all the organizational assets in their possession upon termination of their job, contract or agreement

iso-2013-a-8-2-1.requirements: Information should be classification in the basis of their legal provisions, criticality, and vulnerability to unwanted release or alteration

iso-2013-a-8-2-2.requirements: In accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organization, an adequate set of methods for labelling information should be established and implemented.

iso-2013-a-8-2-3.requirements: Handling of assets in accordance with the organization’s information classification scheme should be developed and implemented.

iso-2013-a-8-3-1.requirements: Procedures shall be implemented for the management of removable media in accordance with the classification scheme adopted by the organization.

iso-2013-a-8-3-2.requirements: When not required by specific protocols, media should be disposed of securely.

iso-2013-a-8-3-3.requirements: Information media should be protected from unauthorized access, misuse or corruption during transportation.

iso-2013-a-9-1-1.requirements: An access control policy with supporting business and information security requirements should be established, documented, and reviewed.

iso-2013-a-9-1-2.control: Access to networks and network services
iso-2013-a-9-1-2.requirements: Only network and network facilities which have expressly been approved for use will be made available to users.

iso-2013-a-9-2-1.requirements: Only network and network facilities which have expressly been approved for use will be made available to users.

iso-2013-a-9-2-2.requirements: A formal process for granting access to users should be put in place to grant or remove access privileges for all categories of users to all systems and services.

iso-2013-a-9-2-3.requirements: A structured authorizing procedure in accordance with the appropriate access management policies should monitor the allocation and usage of delegated access privileges.

iso-2013-a-9-2-4.control: Management of secret authentication information of users
iso-2013-a-9-2-4.requirements: A structured management process should control the allocation of secret authentication information.

iso-2013-a-9-2-5.requirements: Access rights of users should be reviewed regularly by asset owners.

iso-2013-a-9-2-6.control: Removal or adjustment of access rights
iso-2013-a-9-2-6.requirements: Access rights of all employees and external users to information and information processing facilities should be waived upon termination of jobs, contract or agreement, or changed upon adjustment.

iso-2013-a-9-3-1.control: Use of secret authentication information of users
iso-2013-a-9-3-1.requirements: Use of secret authentication information should be allowed for users to follow the organization’s practices.

iso-2013-a-9-4-1.requirements: Access controls should be based on individual requirements for business applications and in compliance with a specified access control policy.

iso-2013-a-9-4-2.requirements: Access to systems and applications should be controlled by a secure log-on procedure when required by the Access Control Policy.

iso-2013-a-9-4-3.requirements: Password management systems should be cooperative to ensure the quality of the passwords.

iso-2013-a-9-4-4.requirements: The use of utility programs that could bypass system and application controls should be limited and tightly controlled.

iso-2013-a-9-4-5.control: Access control to program source code
iso-2013-a-9-4-5.requirements: Access should be limited to the source code of the program.

iso-2013-a-10-1-1.control: Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
iso-2013-a-10-1-1.requirements: A policy on the use of cryptographic controls for protection of information shall be developed and implemented.

iso-2013-a-10-1-2.requirements: A policy on the use, security, and lifetime of cryptographic keys should be created and enforced over their entire life cycle.

iso-2013-a-11-1-1.requirements: Security perimeters should be established in order to secure areas that contain either sensitive or confidential information and information processing facilities.

iso-2013-a-11-1-2.requirements: Appropriate access controls should protect places to ensure that only authorized employees are allowed access.

iso-2013-a-11-1-3.requirements: Physical security should be designed and implemented for the offices, rooms, and facilities.

iso-2013-a-11-1-4.control: Protecting against external and environmental threats
iso-2013-a-11-1-4.requirements: Physical protection should be designed and applied against natural disasters, malicious attacks or accidents.

iso-2013-a-11-1-5.requirements: Procedures should be designed and implemented for working in safe areas.

iso-2013-a-11-1-6.requirements: It is important to track and, where possible, differentiate between access points such as the distribution and loading areas and other locations in order to avoid unauthorized access by unauthorized persons to the premises.

iso-2013-a-11-2-1.requirements: To mitigate the risk of environmental hazards, risks, and unauthorized access, the equipment should be sited and secured.

iso-2013-a-11-2-2.requirements: Equipment should be secured against power failures and other disruptions caused by the supporting infrastructure failures.

iso-2013-a-11-2-3.requirements: Cable for power and telecommunications that carry data or support services should be safeguarded against interception, interference, or damage.

iso-2013-a-11-2-4.requirements: To ensure its continued availability and integrity, the equipment should be correctly maintained.

iso-2013-a-11-2-5.requirements: Without prior authorization, equipment, information, or software should not be taken off-site.

iso-2013-a-11-2-6.control: Security of equipment and assets off-premises
iso-2013-a-11-2-6.requirements: The security of off-site assets should be applied to the various risks of working outside the premises of the organization in mind.

iso-2013-a-11-2-7.control: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
iso-2013-a-11-2-7.requirements: To avoid the removal or overriding of sensitive data and software by the disposal or reuse of any device containing storage medium, all devices must be reviewed.

iso-2013-a-11-2-8.requirements: Unattended equipment should be adequately protected by users.

iso-2013-a-11-2-9.control: Clear desk and clear screen policy
iso-2013-a-11-2-9.requirements: A clear desk policy should be adopted for papers and removable storage media and a clear screen policy should be applied to information processing facilities.

iso-2013-a-12-1-1.requirements: Operating procedures should be documented and accessed by all users in need.

iso-2013-a-12-1-2.requirements: Changes in the organization, organizational procedures, information management facilities, and information security systems should be controlled.

iso-2013-a-12-1-3.requirements: In order to ensure necessary system performance, the use of resources should be monitored, adapted, and projected based on future capacity requirements.

iso-2013-a-12-1-4.control: Separation of development, testing and operational environments
iso-2013-a-12-1-4.requirements: It is important to define and enforce the degree of separation between organizational, testing and development environments needed to avoid operational problems.

iso-2013-a-12-2-1.requirements: In combination with appropriate user awareness, the detection, prevention, and recovery controls to protect against malware should be implemented.

iso-2013-a-12-3-1.requirements: In accordance with the agreed backup policy, copies of records, program and device images shall be collected and regularly tested.

iso-2013-a-12-4-1.requirements: Event logs should be produced, retained, and regularly reviewed to record user activities, exceptions, defects, and information security events.

iso-2013-a-12-4-2.requirements: Logging and log information should be secure from intrusion and unauthorized access.

iso-2013-a-12-4-3.requirements: The activity of the System Manager and System Operator is to be logged, and the logs kept safe and monitored closely.

iso-2013-a-12-4-4.requirements: Clocks in all related information management systems should be integrated into a single reference time source for an organization or safety domain.

iso-2013-a-12-5-1.control: Installation of software on operational systems
iso-2013-a-12-5-1.requirements: To control the installation of software on operating systems, procedures should be implemented.

iso-2013-a-12-6-1.requirements: Information on technological vulnerabilities of information systems used should be obtained in a timely manner, the exposure of the organization to such vulnerabilities should be assessed and appropriate measures taken to address the risk involved

iso-2013-a-12-6-2.requirements: Users should set and implement rules governing software installation.

iso-2013-a-12-7-1.requirements: The audit criteria and activities related to operating system verification should be carefully prepared and decided in order to reduce business process disturbance.

iso-2013-a-13-1-1.requirements: To protect information in systems and applications, networks should be managed and monitored.

iso-2013-a-13-1-2.requirements: Security protocols, quality of service, and management criteria for all network services, whether in-house or outsourced, should be defined and included in-network services agreements.

iso-2013-a-13-1-3.requirements: Network segregation should be established for information services, users, and information systems.

iso-2013-a-13-2-1.requirements: In order to protect the transferees by using all types of communication facilities, official transfer policies, procedures and controls should be developed.

iso-2013-a-13-2-2.requirements: Agreements should address secure transfers between the organization and outside parties of business information.

iso-2013-a-13-2-3.requirements: Electronic messaging information should be adequately protected.

iso-2013-a-13-2-4.requirements: Information protection requirements of the organization for confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements should be identified, regularly reviewed, and documented.

iso-2013-a-14-1-1.requirements: Information security requirements for new information systems or enhancements to existing information systems should be included

iso-2013-a-14-1-2.control: Securing application services on public networks
iso-2013-a-14-1-2.requirements: Application services which pass through public networks should be protected against fraudulent activities, contract disputes, unauthorized disclosure, and modification.

iso-2013-a-14-1-3.requirements: In order to avoid incomplete transmission, misrouting, unauthorized messaging modification, unauthorized dissemination, unauthorized message replication, or replay, information concerning application service transactions should be covered.

iso-2013-a-14-2-1.requirements: Regulations for software and system development should be laid down and applied to organizational developments.

iso-2013-a-14-2-2.requirements: Changes to processes can be managed through the implementation of structured change control procedures within the software lifecycle.

iso-2013-a-14-2-3.control: Technical review of applications after operating platform changes
iso-2013-a-14-2-3.requirements: In changing operating platforms, critical applications of business should be revised and tested to ensure no adverse impacts on business or security.

iso-2013-a-14-2-4.control: Restrictions on changes to software packages
iso-2013-a-14-2-4.requirements: Software package modifications should be discouraged, restricted to the modifications necessary, and all changes controlled strictly.

iso-2013-a-14-2-5.requirements: In the implementation of any information system implementation project, standards for secure system engineered must be established, documented, maintained, and implemented.

iso-2013-a-14-2-6.requirements: Organizations should create secure development environments and integration efforts for the entire life cycle of system development, and should be adequately protected.

iso-2013-a-14-2-7.requirements: The organization must monitor activity for the development of the outsourced system.

iso-2013-a-14-2-8.requirements: During development, security functionality test should be conducted.

iso-2013-a-14-2-9.requirements: New information systems, enhancements, and updated versions should be equipped with acceptance testing services and related requirements.

iso-2013-a-14-3-1.requirements: Careful collection, security, and review of test data should be performed.

iso-2013-a-15-1-1.control: Information security policy for supplier relationships
iso-2013-a-15-1-1.requirements: The supplier should be agreed with and documented information security requirements related to the risk mitigation of access by suppliers to organizational assets.

iso-2013-a-15-1-2.requirements: Any suppliers that view, process, store, communicate or provide IT infrastructure component information for the organization should be defined and agreed with all applicable information security requirements.

iso-2013-a-15-1-3.control: Information and communication technology supply chain
iso-2013-a-15-1-3.requirements: Supplier agreements will contain provisions to mitigate information security risks associated with IT Services and the product supply chain.

iso-2013-a-15-2-1.control: Monitoring and review of supplier services
iso-2013-a-15-2-1.requirements: Organizations shall monitor, review and audit the provision of service to suppliers on a regular basis.

iso-2013-a-15-2-2.requirements: Change in the provision of services by providers should be managed with the focus on the criticality of enterprise information, systems, processes, and reassessment of risks and should include maintaining and improving existing information security policies, procedures, and controls.

iso-2013-a-16-1-1.requirements: In order to ensure a quick, efficient, and organized response to ISO 27001 Annex : A.16 Information Security Incident Management roles and procedures should be defined.

iso-2013-a-16-1-2.requirements: Information security incidents should be reported as quickly as possible through appropriate management channels.

iso-2013-a-16-1-3.requirements: Any information security vulnerabilities found or suspected in systems or services in which employees and contractors are using the information systems and services of the organization should be recorded and documented.

iso-2013-a-16-1-4.control: Assessment of and decision on information security events
iso-2013-a-16-1-4.requirements: Information security events should be analyzed and determined whether they should be listed as incidents related to information security.

iso-2013-a-16-1-5.requirements: In the context of the documented procedures, information security incidents should be responded to.

iso-2013-a-16-1-6.requirements: To minimize the risk or effect of potential accidents, the experience obtained from the study and mitigation of information security accidents should be used.

iso-2013-a-16-1-7.requirements: The organization will define, obtain, procure and retain information as documentation and implement procedures.

iso-2013-a-17-1-1.requirements: In adverse circumstances, e.g. during a crisis or a catastrophe, the company will determine the information security standards and consistency of information security management.

iso-2013-a-17-1-2.requirements: In order to ensure the necessary degree of consistency of information security to adverse circumstances, the company should define, document, execute, and maintain processes, procedures, and controls.

iso-2013-a-17-1-3.control: Verify, review and evaluate information security continuity
iso-2013-a-17-1-3.requirements: In order to ensure accurate and productive to adverse circumstances, the company must review on-going controls on safety information defined and enforced at regular intervals.

iso-2013-a-17-2-1.requirements: How information processing facilities are implemented with redundancy sufficiency to meet availability requirements. Redundancy refers to implementing, typically, duplicate hardware to ensure availability of information processing systems.

iso-2013-a-18-1-1.control: Identification of applicable legislation and contractual requirements
iso-2013-a-18-1-1.requirements: Each of information systems and organizations should specifically identify, document, and update all relevant statutory, regulatory, contractual requirements, and the approach of the organization towards compliance with these requirements.

iso-2013-a-18-1-2.requirements: Proper procedures will be followed to ensure that the legal, regulatory, and contractual provisions relating to ownership of intellectual property and the use of proprietary software products are complied upon.

iso-2013-a-18-1-3.requirements: In accordance with the provisions to legislative, regulatory, contractual, and business requirements, to protect from loss, destruction, falsification, and unauthorized access and unauthorized release.

iso-2013-a-18-1-4.control: Privacy and protection of personally identifiable information
iso-2013-a-18-1-4.requirements: Privacy and protection of personal data should be guaranteed, as required, in applicable laws and regulations.

iso-2013-a-18-1-5.requirements: In accordance with all relevant agreements, legislation, and regulations, cryptographic controls must be used.

iso-2013-a-18-2-1.requirements: Each of these information systems and organizations should specifically identify, document, and update all relevant statutory, regulatory, contractual requirements, and the approach of the organization towards compliance with these requirements.

iso-2013-a-18-2-2.control: Compliance with security policies and standards
iso-2013-a-18-2-2.requirements: Proper procedures will be followed to ensure that the legal, regulatory, and contractual provisions relating to ownership of intellectual property and the use of proprietary software products are complied upon.

iso-2013-a-18-2-3.requirements: Information systems for compliance with the Information Security Policies and practices of an organization should be periodically reviewed.
